event_description: An airborne camera orbits a large, four-engine transport aircraft (Ilyushin Il-76) parked on an airport apron. The camera performs significant perspective changes, transitioning from a side profile to a top-down view. The EO view shows a completely static aircraft. The IR view reveals an overall cold thermal signature with no localized hotspots on the four engines, cooperatively confirming an inactive, cold-soak state.

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with fixed apron position and cold engines; apron markings and perspective change nearby
options.B: the object with apron markings and perspective change; fixed apron position and cold engines nearby
options.C: the object with apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; runway, taxiway, or apron markings nearby
options.D: the object with runway, taxiway, or apron markings; camera orbit or scale change nearby

qa.evidence_note: the aircraft stays static while perspective changes around it

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which fixed-state cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: apron markings and perspective change; view scale or angle changes
options.B: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; view scale or angle changes
options.C: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; view scale or angle changes
options.D: fixed apron position and cold engines; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the aircraft stays static while perspective changes around it

qa.question: In IR, which contrast pairing is visible near the object?

options.A: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry
options.B: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; apron markings and perspective change
options.C: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; fixed apron position and cold engines
options.D: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; runway, taxiway, or apron markings

qa.evidence_note: all four engines remain cold without localized hotspots

qa.question: Which nearby cue creates a same-scene confusion?

options.A: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; near the object
options.B: fixed apron position and cold engines; near the object
options.C: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; near the object
options.D: apron markings and perspective change; near the object

qa.evidence_note: interpreting camera orbit as taxiing or engine activity

options.C: EO: apron markings and perspective change; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.D: EO: fixed apron position and cold engines; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: the aircraft stays static while perspective changes around it; IR: all four engines remain cold without localized hotspots

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
